title: Belloa nivea
taxonomy: Belloa nivea is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Belloa, which contains various species of flowering plants. The species Belloa nivea is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Belloa nivea is further classified as a member of the subtribe Mutisioideae. Its classification is closely related to other plants in the same genus and family.

intro: Belloa nivea, also known as White Belloa or White Cordia, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Belloa nivea has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has confirmed its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Belloa nivea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Belloa nivea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
